title: Time-travel debugger over the event-store oplog
purpose: The differentiated showcase (RM-027). The event store is already a complete oplog, deterministic replay already reconstructs exact state, and the resolver already computes expected-vs-found on a non-determinism fault — so a Golem/Temporal-class time-travel debugger is nearly free on the data side. This brief adds the lens, reading the existing history and replay, never a second store (ADR-007, P6).
task: Add a debugging lens over what the engine already records. In: an engine surface that exposes, per event, the workflow-visible state projection and the recorded now()/random() values (computed from existing history and replay), and the divergent command on a NonDeterminismError; and a dashboard scrubber that steps a run event-by-event and offers a what-if re-run from a chosen event via the existing aion/testing replay path. Out: any new persistence or debug log, and any change to the event schema.

requirements.title: Per-event state projection and determinism diff from history
requirements.spec: WHEN inspecting a run, THE SYSTEM SHALL expose, per event, the workflow-visible state projection and the recorded now() and random() values, computed from the existing event history and deterministic replay. WHEN a NonDeterminismError exists for the run, THE SYSTEM SHALL expose the divergent command as expected-vs-found at the sequence. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT maintain a parallel mutable state store.
acceptance: A per-event workflow-visible state projection is returned for a recorded run, computed from history and replay with no second store (C20).
The recorded now() and random() values for each step are exposed alongside the state.
An injected determinism fault surfaces the exact divergent command (expected vs found at the sequence) the resolver computes.

requirements.title: Dashboard scrubber and what-if re-run
requirements.spec: WHEN an operator opens a run in the dashboard, THE SYSTEM SHALL let them step the run event-by-event and run a what-if re-run from a chosen event with a mocked outcome via the existing aion/testing replay path.
acceptance: The scrubber steps a recorded run event-by-event, showing the projected state at each step.
A what-if re-run forks from a chosen event with a mocked outcome and reports the resulting path, using the existing replay harness.
The scrubber reads the existing event history; no debug-only log is introduced (C16).

boundaries: SHALL NOT introduce a parallel debug log or state store — time-travel reads existing history and replay (ADR-007).
SHALL NOT change the Event schema or the recorded payloads.
SHALL NOT overlap with RM-007's per-run event timeline list view beyond consuming the same history.
verification: Validated against a recorded run and an injected determinism fault.
CN5 asserted: no second source of truth — projections come from history and replay.
